activities.title: To measure the resistance and impedance of an inductor with or without iron core.
activities.details: Measure resistance and impedance using appropriate equipment.

activities.title: To measure resistance, voltage (AC/DC), current (AC) and check continuity of a given circuit using multimeter.
activities.details: Use multimeter to measure various electrical parameters.

activities.title: To assemble a household circuit comprising three bulbs, three (on/off) switches, a fuse and a power source.
activities.details: Assemble components to create a functional household circuit.

activities.title: To assemble the components of a given electrical circuit.
activities.details: Assemble specified electrical components.

activities.title: To study the variation in potential drop with length of a wire for a steady current.
activities.details: Investigate relationship between potential drop and wire length.

activities.title: To draw the diagram of a given open circuit comprising at least a battery, resistor/rheostat, key, ammeter and voltmeter. Mark the components that are not connected in proper order and correct the circuit and also the circuit diagram.
activities.details: Identify errors in a given open circuit diagram and rectify them.

activities.title: To identify a diode, an LED, a resistor and a capacitor from a mixed collection of such items.
activities.chapter_name: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
activities.details: Identify electronic components from a mixture.

activities.title: Use of multimeter to see the unidirectional flow of current in case of a diode and an LED and check whether a given electronic component (e.g., diode) is in working order.
activities.chapter_name: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
activities.details: Test components for functionality using multimeter.

activities.title: To study effect of intensity of light (by varying distance of the source) on an LDR.
activities.chapter_name: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
activities.details: Observe how light intensity affects Light Dependent Resistor.

activities.title: To observe refraction and lateral deviation of a beam of light incident obliquely on a glass slab.
activities.details: Observe optical phenomena in a glass slab.

activities.title: To observe diffraction of light due to a thin slit.
activities.details: Observe diffraction patterns.

activities.title: To study the nature and size of the image formed by a (i) convex lens, or (ii) concave mirror, on a screen by using a candle and a screen (for different distances of the candle from the lens/mirror).
activities.details: Study image formation characteristics.

activities.title: To obtain a lens combination with the specified focal length by using two lenses from the given set of lenses.
activities.details: Combine lenses to achieve specific focal lengths.

projects.title: To study various factors on which the internal resistance/EMF of a cell depends.
projects.details: Investigatory project analyzing factors affecting cell properties.

projects.title: To study the variations in current flowing in a circuit containing an LDR because of a variation in (a) the power of the incandescent lamp, used to 'illuminate' the LDR (keeping all the lamps at a fixed distance). (b) the distance of an incandescent lamp (of fixed power) used to 'illuminate' the LDR.
projects.chapter_name: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
projects.details: Investigatory project on LDR current variations.

projects.title: To find the refractive indices of (a) water (b) oil (transparent) using a plane mirror, an equiconvex lens (made from a glass of known refractive index) and an adjustable object needle.
projects.details: Investigatory project to determine refractive indices.

projects.title: To investigate the relation between the ratio of (i) output and input voltage and (ii) number of turns in the secondary coil and primary coil of a self-designed transformer.
projects.unit_name: Unit IV: Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
projects.details: Investigatory project on transformer design.

projects.title: To investigate the dependence of the angle of deviation on the angle of incidence using a hollow prism filled one by one, with different transparent fluids.
projects.details: Investigatory project on light deviation.

projects.title: To estimate the charge induced on each one of the two identical Styrofoam (or pith) balls suspended in a vertical plane by making use of Coulomb's law.
projects.details: Investigatory project on electrostatic charge estimation.

projects.title: To study the factor on which the self-inductance of a coil depends by observing the effect of this coil, when put in series with a resistor/(bulb) in a circuit fed up by an A.C. source of adjustable frequency.
projects.unit_name: Unit IV: Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
projects.details: Investigatory project on self-inductance factors.

projects.title: To study the earth's magnetic field using a compass needle -bar magnet by plotting magnetic field lines and tangent galvanometer.
projects.unit_name: Unit III: Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
projects.details: Investigatory project on Earth's magnetic field.

activities.title: To study effect of intensity of light (by varying distance of the source) on an LDR.
activities.chapter_name: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
activities.details: Observe how light intensity affects Light Dependent Resistor.

activities.title: To observe refraction and lateral deviation of a beam of light incident obliquely on a glass slab.
activities.details: Observe optical phenomena in a glass slab.

activities.title: To observe diffraction of light due to a thin slit.
activities.details: Observe diffraction patterns.

activities.title: To study the nature and size of the image formed by a (i) convex lens, or (ii) concave mirror, on a screen by using a candle and a screen (for different distances of the candle from the lens/mirror).
activities.details: Study image formation characteristics.

activities.title: To obtain a lens combination with the specified focal length by using two lenses from the given set of lenses.
activities.details: Combine lenses to achieve specific focal lengths.

projects.title: To study various factors on which the internal resistance/EMF of a cell depends.
projects.details: Investigatory project analyzing factors affecting cell properties.

projects.title: To study the variations in current flowing in a circuit containing an LDR because of a variation in (a) the power of the incandescent lamp, used to 'illuminate' the LDR (keeping all the lamps at a fixed distance). (b) the distance of an incandescent lamp (of fixed power) used to 'illuminate' the LDR.
projects.chapter_name: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
projects.details: Investigatory project on LDR current variations.

projects.title: To find the refractive indices of (a) water (b) oil (transparent) using a plane mirror, an equiconvex lens (made from a glass of known refractive index) and an adjustable object needle.
projects.details: Investigatory project to determine refractive indices.

projects.title: To investigate the relation between the ratio of (i) output and input voltage and (ii) number of turns in the secondary coil and primary coil of a self-designed transformer.
projects.unit_name: Unit IV: Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
projects.details: Investigatory project on transformer design.

projects.title: To investigate the dependence of the angle of deviation on the angle of incidence using a hollow prism filled one by one, with different transparent fluids.
projects.details: Investigatory project on light deviation.

projects.title: To estimate the charge induced on each one of the two identical Styrofoam (or pith) balls suspended in a vertical plane by making use of Coulomb's law.
projects.details: Investigatory project on electrostatic charge estimation.

projects.title: To study the factor on which the self-inductance of a coil depends by observing the effect of this coil, when put in series with a resistor/(bulb) in a circuit fed up by an A.C. source of adjustable frequency.
projects.unit_name: Unit IV: Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
projects.details: Investigatory project on self-inductance factors.

projects.title: To study the earth's magnetic field using a compass needle -bar magnet by plotting magnetic field lines and tangent galvanometer.
projects.unit_name: Unit III: Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
projects.details: Investigatory project on Earth's magnetic field.

internal_assessment.details: 30 minutes duration. Total of 15 questions. Student answers any 10.

components.type: Two experiments one from each section
Viva on experiments, activities and project

items: To determine resistivity of two / three wires by plotting a graph for potential difference versus current.
To find resistance of a given wire / standard resistor using metre bridge.
To verify the laws of combination (series) of resistances using a metre bridge OR To verify the laws of combination (parallel) of resistances using a metre bridge.
To determine resistance of a galvanometer by half-deflection method and to find its figure of merit.
To convert the given galvanometer into a voltmeter of desired range and to verify the same OR To convert the given galvanometer into an ammeter of desired range and to verify the same.
To find the frequency of AC mains with a sonometer.
To find the value of v for different values of u in case of a concave mirror and to find the focal length.
To find the focal length of a convex mirror, using a convex lens.
To find the focal length of a convex lens by plotting graphs between u and v or between 1/u and 1/v .
To find the focal length of a concave lens, using a convex lens.
To determine angle of minimum deviation for a given prism by plotting a graph between angle of incidence and angle of deviation.
To determine refractive index of a glass slab using a travelling microscope.
To find the refractive index of a liquid using convex lens and plane mirror.
To find the refractive index of a liquid using a concave mirror and a plane mirror.
To draw the I-V characteristic curve for a p-n junction diode in forward and reverse bias.
To measure the resistance and impedance of an inductor with or without iron core.
To measure resistance, voltage (AC/DC), current (AC) and check continuity of a given circuit using multimeter.
To assemble a household circuit comprising three bulbs, three (on/off) switches, a fuse and a power source.
To assemble the components of a given electrical circuit.
To study the variation in potential drop with length of a wire for a steady current.

To draw the diagram of a given open circuit comprising at least a battery, resistor/rheostat, key, ammeter and voltmeter. Mark the components that are not connected in proper order and correct the circuit.
To identify a diode, an LED, a resistor and a capacitor from a mixed collection of such items.
Use of multimeter to see the unidirectional flow of current in case of a diode and an LED and check whether a given electronic component is in working order.
To study effect of intensity of light (by varying distance of the source) on an LDR.
To observe refraction and lateral deviation of a beam of light incident obliquely on a glass slab.
To observe diffraction of light due to a thin slit.
To study the nature and size of the image formed by a (i) convex lens, or (ii) concave mirror, on a screen by using a candle and a screen.
To obtain a lens combination with the specified focal length by using two lenses from the given set of lenses.

project_assessment.requirements: The record to be submitted must include a report of the project carried out by the students.
suggested_topics: To study various factors on which the internal resistance/EMF of a cell depends.
To study the variations in current flowing in a circuit containing an LDR because of variation in power or distance of incandescent lamp.
To find the refractive indices of water and oil using specific optical setups.
To investigate the relation between voltage ratio/turns ratio and number of turns in a self-designed transformer.
To investigate the dependence of angle of deviation on angle of incidence using hollow prism with different fluids.
To estimate charge induced on two identical Styrofoam balls suspended vertically.
To study factors affecting self-inductance of a coil by observing effect when put in series with resistor/bulb fed by AC source.
To study earth's magnetic field using compass needle-bar magnet and tangent galvanometer.

competency_groups.title: Remembering and Understanding Competencies
competency_groups.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
competencies: Recall facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers related to Physics topics such as Electric Charges, Coulomb's Law, Gauss's Theorem, Ohm's Law, Kirchhoff's Rules, Biot-Savart Law, Ampere's Law, Faraday's Laws, Lenz's Law, Lens Maker's Formula, Photoelectric Effect, Bohr Model, and Nuclear Fission/Fusion.

Organize, compare, translate, interpret, give descriptions of physical phenomena including Electric Fields, Potential Difference, Capacitance, Current Electricity characteristics, Magnetic Effects of Current, Electromagnetic Induction, Optics principles (Reflection/Refraction), Wave Optics concepts, Dual Nature of Radiation, and Atomic/Nuclear structures.
State main ideas regarding the transverse nature of electromagnetic waves, properties of magnetic materials, behavior of semiconductors in p-n junctions, and characteristics of optical instruments.

competency_groups.title: Application Competencies
competency_groups.description: Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
competencies: Apply Coulomb's Law and superposition principle to calculate forces between multiple charges or continuous charge distributions.
Use Gauss's theorem to find electric fields for infinitely long straight wires, infinite plane sheets, and spherical shells.

Calculate electrical energy, power, resistivity, conductivity, and potential differences in circuits involving Ohm's law, internal resistance of cells, and combinations of cells/resistors (series/parallel).
Apply Biot-Savart Law and Ampere's Law to determine magnetic fields due to current-carrying loops and solenoids.
Calculate force on moving charges/currents in magnetic/electric fields, torque on dipoles, and sensitivity of galvanometers.
Analyze LCR circuits for resonance, impedance, reactance, power factor, and wattless currents.
Apply lens formulas (thin lens, mirror formula) to determine image properties, magnification, and focal lengths in optical instruments like microscopes and telescopes.
Use de-Broglie relation and Einstein's photoelectric equation for particle nature of light problems.
Calculate binding energy per nucleon variations and analyze nuclear fission/fusion processes.

competency_groups.title: Analysis Competencies
competency_groups.description: Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.
competencies: Identify motives for charge distribution behaviors (e.g., free vs bound charges, dielectric polarization) based on material properties.
Infer the relationship between drift velocity, mobility, and electric current from microscopic models of conduction.
Analyze causes behind temperature dependence of resistance in conductors and semiconductors.
Examine evidence supporting generalizations about magnetic field lines for bar magnets versus solenoids.
Break down complex optical systems (lens combinations) to determine equivalent focal lengths and image formation characteristics.
Differentiate between para-, dia- and ferro-magnetic substances based on their response to external fields and temperature effects.

competency_groups.title: Evaluation Competencies
competency_groups.description: Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria.
competencies: Evaluate the validity of experimental results in practicals (e.g., resistivity determination, focal length measurement) against theoretical expectations.
Judge the suitability of different optical instruments for specific applications based on magnifying power and design constraints.
Assess the quality of circuit designs involving rectifiers or transformers based on efficiency and component selection criteria.

competency_groups.title: Creation Competencies
competency_groups.description: Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.
competencies: Propose alternative experimental setups for determining physical constants (e.g., refractive index, internal resistance) using available apparatus.
Design circuits incorporating specific components (diodes, capacitors, inductors) to achieve desired functions like rectification or filtering.
Synthesize knowledge of electromagnetic spectrum characteristics to propose applications for different frequency bands.

activities.title: To assemble the components of a given electrical circuit.
activities.details: Assemble specified electrical components.

activities.title: To study the variation in potential drop with length of a wire for a steady current.
activities.details: Investigate relationship between potential drop and wire length.

activities.title: To draw the diagram of a given open circuit comprising at least a battery, resistor/rheostat, key, ammeter and voltmeter. Mark the components that are not connected in proper order and correct the circuit and also the circuit diagram.
activities.details: Identify errors in a given open circuit diagram and rectify them.

activities.title: To identify a diode, an LED, a resistor and a capacitor from a mixed collection of such items.
activities.chapter_name: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
activities.details: Identify electronic components from a mixture.

activities.title: Use of multimeter to see the unidirectional flow of current in case of a diode and an LED and check whether a given electronic component (e.g., diode) is in working order.
activities.chapter_name: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
activities.details: Test components for functionality using multimeter.

activities.title: To study effect of intensity of light (by varying distance of the source) on an LDR.
activities.chapter_name: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
activities.details: Observe how light intensity affects Light Dependent Resistor.

activities.title: To observe refraction and lateral deviation of a beam of light incident obliquely on a glass slab.
activities.details: Observe optical phenomena in a glass slab.

activities.title: To observe diffraction of light due to a thin slit.
activities.details: Observe diffraction patterns.

activities.title: To study the nature and size of the image formed by a (i) convex lens, or (ii) concave mirror, on a screen by using a candle and a screen (for different distances of the candle from the lens/mirror).
activities.details: Study image formation characteristics.

activities.title: To obtain a lens combination with the specified focal length by using two lenses from the given set of lenses.
activities.details: Combine lenses to achieve specific focal lengths.

projects.title: To study various factors on which the internal resistance/EMF of a cell depends.
projects.details: Investigatory project analyzing factors affecting cell properties.

projects.title: To study the variations in current flowing in a circuit containing an LDR because of a variation in (a) the power of the incandescent lamp, used to 'illuminate' the LDR (keeping all the lamps at a fixed distance). (b) the distance of an incandescent lamp (of fixed power) used to 'illuminate' the LDR.
projects.chapter_name: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
projects.details: Investigatory project on LDR current variations.

projects.title: To find the refractive indices of (a) water (b) oil (transparent) using a plane mirror, an equiconvex lens (made from a glass of known refractive index) and an adjustable object needle.
projects.details: Investigatory project to determine refractive indices.

projects.title: To investigate the relation between the ratio of (i) output and input voltage and (ii) number of turns in the secondary coil and primary coil of a self-designed transformer.
projects.unit_name: Unit IV: Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
projects.details: Investigatory project on transformer design.

projects.title: To investigate the dependence of the angle of deviation on the angle of incidence using a hollow prism filled one by one, with different transparent fluids.
projects.details: Investigatory project on light deviation.

projects.title: To estimate the charge induced on each one of the two identical Styrofoam (or pith) balls suspended in a vertical plane by making use of Coulomb's law.
projects.details: Investigatory project on electrostatic charge estimation.

projects.title: To study the factor on which the self-inductance of a coil depends by observing the effect of this coil, when put in series with a resistor/(bulb) in a circuit fed up by an A.C. source of adjustable frequency.
projects.unit_name: Unit IV: Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
projects.details: Investigatory project on self-inductance factors.

projects.title: To study the earth's magnetic field using a compass needle -bar magnet by plotting magnetic field lines and tangent galvanometer.
projects.unit_name: Unit III: Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
projects.details: Investigatory project on Earth's magnetic field.

competency_weightage: Remembering: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Understanding: Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas
Applying: Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Analysing: Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations

internal_assessment: Practical Examination for Visually Impaired Students: Max Marks 30, Time 2 hours

internal_assessment.details: 30 minutes duration. Total of 15 questions. Student answers any 10.

components.type: Two experiments one from each section
Viva on experiments, activities and project

items: To determine resistivity of two / three wires by plotting a graph for potential difference versus current.
To find resistance of a given wire / standard resistor using metre bridge.
To verify the laws of combination (series) of resistances using a metre bridge OR To verify the laws of combination (parallel) of resistances using a metre bridge.
To determine resistance of a galvanometer by half-deflection method and to find its figure of merit.
To convert the given galvanometer into a voltmeter of desired range and to verify the same OR To convert the given galvanometer into an ammeter of desired range and to verify the same.
To find the frequency of AC mains with a sonometer.
To find the value of v for different values of u in case of a concave mirror and to find the focal length.
To find the focal length of a convex mirror, using a convex lens.
To find the focal length of a convex lens by plotting graphs between u and v or between 1/u and 1/v .
To find the focal length of a concave lens, using a convex lens.
To determine angle of minimum deviation for a given prism by plotting a graph between angle of incidence and angle of deviation.
To determine refractive index of a glass slab using a travelling microscope.
To find the refractive index of a liquid using convex lens and plane mirror.
To find the refractive index of a liquid using a concave mirror and a plane mirror.
To draw the I-V characteristic curve for a p-n junction diode in forward and reverse bias.
To measure the resistance and impedance of an inductor with or without iron core.
To measure resistance, voltage (AC/DC), current (AC) and check continuity of a given circuit using multimeter.
To assemble a household circuit comprising three bulbs, three (on/off) switches, a fuse and a power source.
To assemble the components of a given electrical circuit.
To study the variation in potential drop with length of a wire for a steady current.

To draw the diagram of a given open circuit comprising at least a battery, resistor/rheostat, key, ammeter and voltmeter. Mark the components that are not connected in proper order and correct the circuit.
To identify a diode, an LED, a resistor and a capacitor from a mixed collection of such items.
Use of multimeter to see the unidirectional flow of current in case of a diode and an LED and check whether a given electronic component is in working order.
To study effect of intensity of light (by varying distance of the source) on an LDR.
To observe refraction and lateral deviation of a beam of light incident obliquely on a glass slab.
To observe diffraction of light due to a thin slit.
To study the nature and size of the image formed by a (i) convex lens, or (ii) concave mirror, on a screen by using a candle and a screen.
To obtain a lens combination with the specified focal length by using two lenses from the given set of lenses.

project_assessment.requirements: The record to be submitted must include a report of the project carried out by the students.
suggested_topics: To study various factors on which the internal resistance/EMF of a cell depends.
To study the variations in current flowing in a circuit containing an LDR because of variation in power or distance of incandescent lamp.
To find the refractive indices of water and oil using specific optical setups.
To investigate the relation between voltage ratio/turns ratio and number of turns in a self-designed transformer.
To investigate the dependence of angle of deviation on angle of incidence using hollow prism with different fluids.
To estimate charge induced on two identical Styrofoam balls suspended vertically.
To study factors affecting self-inductance of a coil by observing effect when put in series with resistor/bulb fed by AC source.
To study earth's magnetic field using compass needle-bar magnet and tangent galvanometer.
